segment-text: July 6, 2018 Shooting Draft
INT. APARTMENT- PARTY- EVENING
A party full of successful looking 30-somthings. BRIDGET, 34, a little tipsy, sits talking to a man who looks to be around her age. He is dressed expensively; Bridget is not.
I wake up in the middle of the night. Drenched in sweat, heart racing. And I reach out my hand to Lindsay's side of the bed, but
boxspring. And I find my way out into the hallway and go into Cooper's room, but he's not there either. And I run down the hall
walk-up in Uptown. And I turn around and a window appears. And I chuck myself right the fuck out of

To kill myself. Because I realize I never had a family, I never started Wiggler, I've got no equity, no job, I'm 34 years old with no savings, no future, totally alone, and I'm filled with shame and self loathing. I hate myself. So I kill myself. Beat.
That's the nightmare, anyway. mean, Jesus, can you imagine? (beat) What do you do?
I'm a server. (beat) At a restaurant.
That's cool. I mean, you're in your 20's. It gets better. I'm 34. (beat)
You look good. (spotting a woman on the other side of the room) Would you excuse me... Yup.
Bridget has noticed a young man, JACE, also underdressed in cheap, wrinkled clothes. He grabs a PBR and heads towards Bridget; they're the two rejects among the shiny people.
I'm Bridget. Jace.
How do you know Amy?
She's a regular at Le Mer.
You own a restaurant?
I'm a server.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - NIGHT
Bridget and Jace have sex, silhouetted in the dark.
I'm gonna come. I'm coming. Me too.
Pull out.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM -MORNING

Bridget scrolls through Facebook on her phone while Jace sleeps. She sees pic after pic of friends' wedding photos, new baby photos, images of success validated with many likes and comments of congratulations. A reminder pops up on her phone: Interview: 1 hour.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
Bridget yawns and looks in the bathroom mirror. She sees finger prints of blood on her cheek.
Hey, I think you might have started your period. There's a little blood...
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM -CONTINUOUS
Bridget steps into the bedroom and sees a crimescene-like stain of blood on the sheets.
...a lot of blood.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM -CONTINUOUS
Bridget and Jace pull the sheets off the bed, trying to contain the blood stain. There is a good natured awkwardness between them.
I'll take care of pillows, if you
Yeah, let's maybe try to isolate it(she notices his face) You...you have some on your face.
Really, where? (he touches his face)
Oh, it's on your hand, too.
Oh, yeah. Ok.
I don't think I had started when you were going down on me.
No, you tasted normal.
Apparently some guys are into that, they call themselves "Bloodhounds." (beat) I'm not into that.
Me either. Like, sex on your period I'm totally fine withBRIDGET Sure, put a towel down.
But not like, oral. If it can be avoided.
It's really hard to take anything you say seriously with that much blood on your face.
(playfully) I swear to you I'm not a bloodhound.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BATHROOM - A FEW MINUTES LATER
Bridget brushes her teeth while Jace showers. A reminder alert goes off on her phone. Shit!
She reaches into the shower and turns it off.
You gotta go! Why?
(throwing a towel to him) I have a job interview this morning. For what?
(rushing out of the room) To be a nanny.
You must really like kids. I don't!
Can I see you again?
INT. BRIDGET'S CAR - DAY

Bridget drives north on Ridge Avenue and passes Northwestern University's arch. She looks at it, then looks away. Her phone dings with a text from Jace: A picture of a hound dog with ketchup on its snout that says, "good luck from this Bloodhound." Bridget laughs. She looks up to see a minivan stopped at a light in front of her car. She slams on the brakes. She sees a sticker with stick figures of a family (two parents, two kids, and a dog) on the back windshield of the minivan.
EXT. EVANSTON - DAY

Bridget drives through streets lined with increasingly palatial houses. Bridget slows in front of a not overly-huge, but still obviously pricey house. She parks at a distance where her car can't be seen from the house.
EXT. EVANSTON NEIGHBORHOOD - DAY

Bridget walks up the front path toward the front door of the house. There are "Black Lives Matter" and "Hate Has No Home Here" signs in the windows. She sees the face of a little girl appear in an upstairs window, looking down on her. Bridget rings the doorbell. MAYA, a very pregnant woman in her early 40's, answers the door.
You must be Bridget.
Maya hugs Bridget. Bridget does not hug back.
INT. MAYA AND ANNIE'S HOUSE - A FEW MINUTES LATER
Bridget sits across the dining room table from Maya and Annie, also in her early 40's.
You'd start in June when Frannie gets out for summer, and in August we'd set you free again.
I'll be gone most of the time. My firm doesn't give maternity leave unless you do the actual birthing.
I'll be working some from home, but our doctor says I should build in extra rest time because of my age.
Bridget spies a small girl's head poking around the staircase.
I have a "geriatric pregnancy."
The child disappears back around the corner.
If you're over 35, that is the medical name...
Probably coined by some geriatric white man. You've nannied before?
Not full time. I've baby-sitted. Sat.
Dana mentioned you had siblings?
One brother. 6 years younger.
That'll be the age difference for Frannie and Wally! We were worried that might be too far apart for them to be friendsANNIE Are you and your brother close?
Not really. He has, like, a job and a house, he's very responsible...we don't have much in common.
Annie and Maya exchange a small glance.
Did you have trouble finding the house?
INT. MAYA'S HOUSE - STAIRWAY - A FEW MINUTES LATER

Maya, Annie, and Bridget climb the stairs to the second floor. There are photos of Frances, family photos, and a large painting of Mary and baby Jesus lining the wall. Bridget recognizes the painting as "Our Lady of Perpetual Help."
You can be creeped out by that, I am.
You Catholic?
Fallen. I went to Immaculate Conception from kindergarten to eighth grade.
Maya wanted Frannie to go to Catholic school, but I put my foot down.
Probably a good idea if you don't want her to hate the church. (beat) I don't hate it...
INT. MAYA'S HOUSE - FRANCES' BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
Maya opens the door and gestures for Bridget to go in.
(whispering)

I wonder where Frannie is... Bridget looks around the room. There are piles of toys, a half closed closet, bunched up sheets on the bed. A kid could be hiding anywhere, and it's creepy af. Bridget pulls up a blanket to reveal a stuffed animal hiding underneath. She checks behind an armoir. She approaches a closet and opens it, revealing a very small 6 year old girl, FRANCES, standing in the corner, staring unblinking up at
Hello. (beat) Did I win?
FRANCES
Did my mom help you?
Bridget looks to Maya. Maya shakes her head.
Frances walks over to a pile of toys and begins silently playing.
Let's give you a few minutes to get to know each other.
I actually can't stay longMaya and Annie exit and close the door. Bridget stares at Frances.
What are you playing? (referring to a stuffed armadillo) Who's this? (MORE)
(silence) Dana told me lots of nice things about you.
Is Dana coming?
No, she moved when she had her baby, remember? But Dana's my best friend, and she thought you and I would get along.
Frances goes back to playing. Bridget doesn't know what to do. She picks up a toy phone.
Hello? Frances? I'm not sure there's a Frances here, let me check. (calling out) Frannie? Frances, you have a very important call!
Frances continues to ignore Bridget.
I'm not sure she can understand me right now, want to talk to her?
Bridget holds the receiver to Frances' ear. Frances freezes. Silence.
(talking into the phone again) See what I mean?
Frances walks to the door and opens it, revealing Annie and Maya listening at the door.
We're done.
EXT. MAYA'S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - DAY

Maya and Annie walk Bridget outside, then step inside and close the door. Bridget walks down the front walkway toward her car. Frances looks down on her from the upstairs window. Bridget waves. Frances closes the curtain without waving.
INT. BRIDGET'S CAR - DAY
Bridget talks on the phone with Dana, who has Bridget on speaker phone while she changes the diaper of a crying baby.
I'm not gonna get itWhy?
'Cause I was honest! I should'a just lied, said I love kids and I'm CPR certifiedThe baby stops crying. Oh my god! What?
BRIDGET
The teepee peepee worked! Now you can't spray peepee into Mommy's face anymore, can you? Can you? Bridget is disgusted.
Did you run into any Northwestern people? No, why...
Be ready. That neighborhood's crawling with people we went to
You went to school with.
You were there for a yearBRIDGET Doesn't count. Oh my god!
He just smiled! It could just be gas, but... (to the baby) Can you smile? Can you smile for mama? Can you smile for mama and Bridget?
I can't see him...
Let me get it on videoBRIDGET Film it laterDANA I don't know if he'll do it againEver?
I gotta go!
Dana has hung up. Bridget drives away, feeling alone.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - DAY
Bridget sits on her bare mattress. She opens her laptop and writes in google's search bar: "What to do with my life". It autofills in "quiz, at 20, at 35, at 50." She clicks on "at 35."
INT. RESTAURANT - EVENING
Bridget rushes around a chaotic restaurant getting people's food and dropping off their drinks. A coworker bumps into her. Bridget grabs her boob. Ow!
INT. RESTAURANT - BATHROOM - EVENING

Bridget checks a new voicemail as she pees. Hi Bridget, this is Maya Rodriguez. Listen, Annie and I were wondering if you might still be available for the summer position. Bridget throwing her apron on the counter and walking toward the door.
EXT. MAYA'S HOUSE - MORNING
Bridget stands on the front porch as Maya opens the door. She's no longer pregnant, and she carries a baby in her arms.
You are an absolute life-saver.
INT. MAYA'S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
The woman we hired came with tons of experience and recommendations, but she was older and kind of
rigid. She had trouble keeping up with FrannieMaya tries to breastfeed Wally, unsuccessfully.

-and Frannie was coming home even more wound up, and I was left with a wild 6 year old and a screaming baby until Annie got home at eight and I thought I might lose my mind if I had to go another week like that.
Bridget is unnerved by Maya's exposed breast. She doesn't know where to look.
So we let her go. Well, Annie did. I couldn't do it.
Wally fusses.
Come on, sweetie, latch. Apparently I have flat nipples. I went 44 years without knowing that, now it's a huge problem.
This is Wally?
Wow.
I know! And he'll get another name when he's confirmed. Wally fusses.
Frannie was such a happy baby. I don't think Wally's sold on life, just yet.
Maya rubs Wally's forehead, attempting to de-furrow it.
Where is Frannie?
INT. MAYA'S HOUSE - FRANCES' ROOM - DAY.
Bridget and Maya stand outside Frances' door, listening. Quiet.
Maya realizes it's too quiet. She opens the door. Frances sits on her bed, rubbing her eyes. The room is completely trashed with every item of clothing, every toy strewn about the room.
I apologize.
EXT. MAYA'S HOUSE - DAY
Maya carries a crying Wally and a closed-up stroller outside as Frances and Bridget follow.
Do you know how to get to the park? I don't trust Frannie to guide you.
I apologize!
I can look it up on my phone.
Maya hands Bridget a 50 dollar bill.
(the Bridget) For snacks. No sugar.
Bridget looks at the money, surprised this is considered "snack money." Maya carries Wally, who is still crying, back into the house.
Bye, Mommy!
Maya shuts the door. Frances is dejected.
I'm Bridget, remember me?
Frances rolls her eyes and walks down the sidewalk toward the
Hold on.
BRIDGET (CONT'D)
Bridget carries the unopened stroller down the stairs and tries, unsuccessfully, to open it.
Do you know how to open this?
I can walk.
Your mom wanted us to bring it.
It's for the baby. Leave it on the porch.
I don't want anyone to steal it.
The crime rate's really low here.
Bridget leaves the stroller on the porch, then joins Frances in walking down the street.
EXT. SIDEWALK - DAY- CONTINUOUS
So, what's your favorite color?
Frances walks more quickly away from Bridget. Slow down.
How old are you?
How old do you think I am?
Why do you think that?
That's how old Linda was, and she was as slow as you. I'm 34.
Do you have kids? No.
Are you married? No.
Do you have a boyfriend? No.
A girlfriend?
No. Do you have a boyfriend or a girlfriend? No.
We have something in common. I'm tired.
We just started walking.
Will you carry me?
Let's go back and get the stroller.
We're almost there, it won't take long if you carry me.
The park is really close?
Frances nods. Frances gets on Bridget's back.
You're sweaty...
EXT. PARK - DAY

Bridget walks into the park. It was not a short walk, and she is drenched in sweat. Frances hops down and runs to the playground. Bridget looks around the park and watches the kids. She does not like kids.
Can I have some money?
Bridget spies an ice cream cart some distance away.
Your mom said no sugar.
We can buy an apple or something on the way home.
Frances grabs Bridget's purse and runs away with it.
BRIDGET (CONT'D)
Bridget grabs Frances by the arm.
Help! She's not my mom! I don't know her! Help!
Bridget turns to see the entire playground looking at them, Bridget's hand still gripping Frances' bicep.
EXT. MAYA'S HOUSE - DAY
Maya, with wet hair and in a towel, opens her front door revealing Bridget, Frances, and a police officer on the front porch.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - NIGHT
Jace is laughing.
I just gotta get through summer...
What'd her mom do?
She started yelling at Frances, but
then Frances started crying and then Maya started crying, and Maya left the room and came back with
Bridget pulls out two fifty dollar bills from her pocket. She "makes it rain" on Jace. Jace playfully rolls around on the two fifties.
Maya was this confident, funny, sexy woman, and now she's this, like...un-showered, bare-boobed, perpetually crying milk machine.
Jace kisses Bridget.
What about this conversation is turning you on?
Unshowered.
They kiss. Jace cups Bridget's breast.
They get swollen before I get my period. I know. You know?
BRIDGET
I pay attention.
Do you prefer them like this?
I like them at all stages.
Bridget rolls her eyes.
INT. DRUGSTORE - DAY

Bridget carries a bottle of wine, Count Chocula, and a half gallon of chocolate milk in a basket. She passes the tampon section and grabs a box. She slows, thinks, and checks her phone calendar. She feels her boobs. She walks to the next aisle and looks at the pregnancy tests. She stares. She selects a box. She walks to the end of the aisle as a TEENAGE GIRL passes her along the way. At the cash register, a MIDDLE AGED MALE EMPLOYEE passes her items under the scanner. His eyes dart up to Bridget when he scans the pregnancy test. Bridget heads toward the exit and looks back at where the Teenage Girl stands and stares at the pregnancy tests, trying to be invisible.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - NIGHT

Bridget sits on her couch, two positive pregnancy tests on the coffee table in front of her. Hey eyes are red, like she's been crying. She finds Dana's name in her text messages. She looks at the last text in their conversation, which is the video of a Dana's newborn. Bridget puts her phone down without texting Dana.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
Jace and Bridget sit on the couch.
I'm for sure getting rid of it. Ok.
(beat) We can talk about other optionsBRIDGET No. Thank you. (beat)
Do you think your birth control, like, malfunctioned?
I'm not on birth control.
You're not?
I've used this... method for like, 8 years. Are you sure you pulled out every time?
I think so.
Your sperm are probably super fast. I honestly thought I couldn't getWhy?
Cause it's never happened before. (Bridget drops her head.) I hear it now. I hear how that sounds. (beat)
I'll go with you. When you go. Ok.
And I'll pay for itBRIDGET -we'll split it.
EXT. MAYA'S HOUSE - DAY
Bridget struggles to open the stroller, slamming it on the ground. Frances walks over and presses one button on the stroller, which opens immediately.
Mama said I have to try and respect you.
Frances sits, and Bridget pushes the stroller.
Where are we going?
The library.
I don't want to be quiet!
We can go to the park after, I just need some quiet time to think.
About your choices? What?
When I'm in time out I'm supposed to think about my choices.
Bridget pushes the stroller over a big bump in the sidewalk.
Do that again.
Bridget pretends there's another big bump and rocks the stroller. Frances laughs. Again!
FRANCES (CONT'D) INT. LIBRAY - DAY
Frances runs into the library past a middle aged LIBRARIAN.
No running, please!
Frances walks toward the kid section. She selects some books and sits. Bridget sits next to her, and pulls out her phone. Frances hands Bridget a book.
You can't read?
Hearing it helps my brain development.
Bridget looks at Frances incredulously. Bridget begins reading a book out loud and becomes instantly bored. She finishes the first page, then skips to the end.
You skipped a bunch.
It's boring until the end.
Start over.
Bridget stares at Frances. Frances stares back, unflinching.
Start. Over.
It's a standoff. After a moment, Bridget hands the book to Frances.
Do your moms leave you here when they go to the bathroom? Of course.
INT. LIBRARY - BATHROOM - DAY

Bridget checks her phone while she pees. There's a text from Jace that reads, "Let me know when the appointment is. And if you want to hang out tonight." Bridget texts back, "8am, tomorrow. See you then." Bridget hears a mom helping her child use the stall next to her. Under the stall, Bridget sees his feet dangling. The child makes grunting, pooping noises.
INT. LIBRAY - DAY

Bridget turns the corner to see that Frances has emptied the contents of Bridget's purse onto the floor around her, including 3 tampons and a few panty liners. She is practicing pushing a tampon through its applicator.
(loudly and excitedly) Are you on your period??
EXT. SIDEWALK - DAY
Bridget pushes Frances in the stroller as Frances sings a song loudly.
Frannie, can you sing a little more quietly, please?
We're outside, I can use my outside voice!!
Bridget shakes the stroller like they went over a bump.
Bridget shakes the stroller.
FRANCES (CONT'D)
Bridget bumps the stroller really hard, vaulting Frances out of the stroller onto the concrete. Shit!
Frances stares down at her scraped palms, which begin to bleed. Frances looks at Bridget, and scream sobs.
INT. MAYA'S HOUSE - DAY.
Maya washes dishes as Frances runs into the house.
She threw me out of the stroller.
We went over some bumps and I forgot to buckle her in. I'm sorry.
Maya looks at Frances' scrapes.
Those aren't bad at all.
She kisses Frances' palms. Wally cries from the other room.
(to Frances) Help Bridget.
Maya goes to Wally's room. Frances walks toward the bathroom.
(to Bridget) Come on.
INT. MAYA'S HOUSE - BATHROOM - DAY.
Frances holds her scraped palms under the faucet. Soap.
Bridget pumps soap into Frances' hands. She helps Frances wash, then dry her hands. Neosporin.
FRANCES (CONT'D)
Bridget rubs a dab onto Frances' scrapes. Frances takes a box of bandaids and takes out a few. Bandaids.
FRANCES (CONT'D)
Bridget helps bandaid Frances' palms. Frances holds up another bandaid.
Where do you want yours?
EXT. CLINIC - MORNING
Jace and Bridget walk up to the doors of a Planned Parenthood type clinic. The doors are locked. They buzz a doorbell on the wall and are buzzed in.
INT. CLINIC - VESTIBULE - DAY.
They walk into a vestibule where an administrator behind bulletproof glass takes both of their IDs and buzzes them into the lobby.
INT. CLINIC - DAY

Bridget and Jace sit in the waiting room. There are all types of women and a few men sitting in the chairs. Bridget reads "Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince." A WOMAN in her mid 30's sits across from Bridget.
First time? What?
The woman points to the book.
dies?
Dumbledore dies?
A nurse pokes her head out of the waiting room door. Bridget D?
I'll be here.
Bridget stands and gives the woman a dirty look. The woman is buried in her phone again.
INT. CLINIC - EXAM ROOM - DAY
Bridget wears a paper gown. She looks at the bandaid on her index finger. A nurse enters.
I understand you're looking to get an abortion today.
Yes. I'm in the market.
We'll start with an ultrasoundBRIDGET I thought I wouldn't need oneNURSE We need it to tell how far along you are. You don't have to look at it.
From Bridget's POV, a picture on the ceiling of a beach and

the ocean, the kind of pic that's supposed to be calming. The Nurse performs the ultrasound, with Bridget on her back, legs in the stirrups. The nurse looks at a monitor that's turned away from Bridget.
Want to know if it's twins? I guess.
It's not. You can sit up.

Bridget is alone in the exam room, getting dressed. She stops when she sees that the nurse has left the ultrasound image up on the monitor. She studies the black and white image. Bridget grabs her phone and takes a picture of the image on the monitor.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - DAY

Bridget and Jace sit across from each other on the couch. A few prescription bottles and a box of super absorbant maxi pads sit on the coffee table in front of them. Bridget stares at her phone, which dings from a Venmo notification from Jace: "$250, for: no whammies."
This is gonna get messy.
I know. I did research. (beat) And we've already encountered blood in our relationship, so a little
We're not in a relationship. ...Oh.
And we can talk about that, if you want to, but not right now-
You're 26. It's those agile little, 26 year old sperm that got us into this in the first place.
And your 34 year old eggs.
Bridget stares daggers at him.
I just mean your eggs are in good shape, too.
Bridget opens one of the bottles and takes two pills out.
I feel like you should have to do something. Like what?
Like give yourself food poisoning or something.
Jace stares at Bridget, unsure if she's serious. Bridget sighs. She places one pill in each cheek and begins to let them dissolve.
I have to keep them here for 20 minutes. Do I look cute?
You look very cute.
I feel cute.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - 20 MINUTES LATER
Jace and Bridget watch TV. Bridget sits up from her reclined position, runs to the bathroom, and shuts the door. Sounds of vomiting. Jace waits outside the bathroom door.
Get away from the door! Turn the volume up really loud and stay in
the other room!
Can I get you anything?
You can get the fuck away from the
door!
Jace runs back to the living room and turns the volume up really loud.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BATHROOM - DAY
Bridget is on the toilet, in pain. She flushes the toilet, then looks back down into the toilet bowl. Nope, still not
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - AFTERNOON
Bridget goes through several super absorbant maxi pads.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - EVENING
Bridget sits in child's pose on her bed, rocking back and forth.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - NIGHT
Jace reads Harry Potter to Bridget. He reads information on "how to care for yourself after an abortion."
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - NIGHT
Jace lies in bed. Bridget enters the room carrying a wad of toilet paper.
Can I show you something? It's really gross.
Bridget opens the wad of toilet paper for him to see.
Do you think that's it?
I don't know.
They both look.
Is that how big it would be?
Online it said it's about the size of a sweet pea.
How big is a sweet pea?
About this size. There's been a lot of blood, though, so this could just be another clot.
They both look.
I hate how they compare it to cute
things. A sweet pea. A raspberry. A
When, really, it's just the size of a rat turd.
Bridget smiles, grateful for the joke.
INT. BRIDGET'S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - NIGHT
Jace is asleep. Bridget lies in bed, awake. She stares at the ceiling.
EXT. MUSIC BUILDING - DAY The outside of a Creative Arts building in Evanston.
Bridget parks outside and gets Frannie and a child-sized guitar out of the backseat.
INT. MUSIC BUILDING - CLASSROOM - DAY

A bunch of kids around Frances' age run around the room, play small sets of drums, etc, while parents watch and chit chat. A middle-aged woman with a clipboard, MARGARET, approaches Bridget and Frances.
Here for Lil' Strummers?
Yes. This is Frances.
Margaret finds the name on the list and checks her off.
Perfect! I'm Ms. Margaret. We have a couple of minutes before class begins, so feel free to play until you hear the welcome song!
(to Bridget) Are you going to stay?
We highly encourage parents to play along with their littles.
I don't know how.
What a perfect opportunity for you to learn! Don't tell me this old
dog can't learn new tricks. Frances giggles.
I just have some important adult stuff I need to take care of.
Bridget gives Margaret a look like "A mom's job is never done" and Margaret nods like, "boy, do I get it."
(to Frannie) You'll have a great time with Ms. Margaret.
Oh, I'm just an administrator, not an artist. Your teacher is Mr. Isaac over there.

Camera cuts to a tall, 40-something man in the corner ISAAC. He is the hottest version of dad-sexy, with salt and pepper hair, and a thick, lustrous beard- the kind that's been oiled and trimmed religiously. He has tattoos peaking out from under his t-shirt, and a weathered-but-cared-for guitar
He's a poet. We sell several of his "chapbooks" downstairs. He's very good. Very...descriptive.
Bridget and Margaret stare at Isaac. Bridget is suspicious of this dude, but also highly attracted to him. Ok...bye.

Frances begins banging on a set of drums. Bridget goes to leave the classroom and turns to look at Isaac again. He smiles at her. Bridget flushes. As she closes the door, she hears Isaac begin to play guitar and sing.
INT. MUSIC BUILDING - BATHROOM - DAY
Bridget replaces her maxi pad. She wraps the used pad in lots of toilet paper, and places it in the trash bin. She checks the mirror to see if the super maxi pad is obvious.
INT. MUSIC BUILDING - STORE ROOM - DAY

Bridget wanders the aisles of the music store in the building. An OLD HIPPIE sits behind the cash register. Bridget sees a collection of books in the window, one of which is "breathe/beat/bang" by Isaac Daniels. She replaces the book and continues to browse. She picks a guitar off the wall. She strums.
That one's too small. You have big hands. Oh.
Long fingers are a sign of creativity and genius.
He picks a larger guitar off the wall and hands it to her. Bridget holds the guitar. She strums and grimaces at the chord self-consciously.
I don't know how.
Your body doesn't yet, but your heart's been playing all your life.
Bridget laughs at what she assumed was a joke. It wasn't.
I'll take it.
That's 450 dollars.
Off Bridget, handing over her debit cared, realizing she
INT. MUSIC BUILDING - CLASSROOM - DAY
Bridget enters the classroom, awkwardly carrying her new guitar, sans case. Frances runs up to Bridget.
Did you steal that?
I bought it.
But you don't have any money.
Isaac approaches Frances and Bridget.
I think we have a little Joan Jet on our hands. Are you Frances's mom?
(insulted) No!
(insulted) No! I just watch her. I'm Bridget.
Bridget reaches out to shake Isaac's hand. Isaac takes her hand and holds it.
Frances showed a natural aptitude today. Don't tell the other kids, but she's my new favorite.
